author: Dr. David Park (PGY-1) / Dr. Maria Castro (PGY-3) / Dr. Sarah Beckman (attending)
content:
INTERNAL MEDICINE PROGRESS NOTE — HOSPITAL DAY 8 (MULTIDISCIPLINARY SYNTHESIS)
Date/Time: 3/22/2027, 8:30 AM (rounding)
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Team: Dr. Sarah Beckman (attending), Dr. Maria Castro (PGY-3), Dr. David Park (PGY-1)
Consultants actively involved: Rheumatology (Dr. Tanaka/Dr. Park), Neurology (Dr. Yang), Dermatology (Dr. Chen), Nutrition (Eun-Ji Lee, RD), Infectious Disease, Psychiatry CL, SLP (Carolyn Wu), Social Work (Diana Mercado, LCSW).
Interpreter: Spanish phone interpreter present at morning interview.

SUBJECTIVE:
Ms. Sandoval reports she 'feels a little better today.' She has been able to swallow her own saliva more comfortably and her face feels 'less stuck.' She slept ~6 hours overnight, the most so far this admission. She did NG-tube feeds overnight without intolerance (no abdominal distension, no diarrhea). Mother reports her face looks 'less puffy' this morning. Patient continues to express low mood and worry about future, but smiled when her younger brother visited yesterday.

Specifically denied: New chest pain, dyspnea worse than baseline, fever, severe headache, focal weakness, vision changes, abdominal pain, vomiting, melena, hematuria.

VITALS (24h):
Tmax 99.4 F (37.4 C), Tcurrent 98.0 F
BP: range 96-108 / 56-66 (improvement from admission 86/52 with cautious volume + albumin support; not orthostatic)
HR: range 88-104 (down from peak 124 on admission)
RR: 16-20
SpO2: 96-99% on room air (off supplemental oxygen since HD3)
Weight 3/22 AM: 37.8 kg (up 2.4 kg from admission 35.4 kg; gain is mix of feeding rebound + fluid; edema gradually decreasing per nursing assessment)
I/O: 24h in 2480 mL (NG feeds + IV + meds), 24h out 1810 mL
Daily weight chart attached to bedside; daily I/O strict.

FOCUSED EXAM:
General: Cachetic but appears improved from HD1. Better color. Mother at bedside.
Neuro: Awake, alert, oriented x 3. Engages in Spanish. Voice still nasal but mildly improved per SLP assessment.
MSK: Strength testing today (with SLP and PT cooperation):
- Hip flexion: 3/5 R, 3/5 L (improved from 2/5)
- Shoulder abduction: 3/5 bilaterally (improved from 2/5)
- Neck flexion: 3-/5 (still significantly weak)
- Knee extension: 4-/5
- Distal: 4+/5 throughout
- Sitting balance: improved; able to sit at edge of bed with two-person assist for 5 minutes
Skin: Scaling dermatosis appears to be peaking — desquamation continues but underlying skin appears less inflamed. Mild improvement in 'flag sign' visibility on more recently-grown proximal hair.
Abdomen: Less distended; mild residual edema in flanks; no new tenderness; bowel sounds normoactive.
Extremities: Pitting edema reduced from +2 to +1 at ankles; sacral edema reduced.
Lungs: Clear; small bilateral basilar dullness consistent with persistent (improving) pleural effusions.
Oropharynx: 3 of original 4 oral ulcers healing; new ulcer not noted.

DIAGNOSTIC RESULTS — KEY UPDATES OVER PAST 48 HOURS:

1. MUSCLE BIOPSY (3/19/2027, FINALIZED 3/22 AM): 
   ACTIVE INFLAMMATORY MYOPATHY with features of BOTH polymyositis (CD8+ endomysial infiltrate invading non-necrotic fibers, diffuse MHC class I upregulation) AND dermatomyositis (perifascicular atrophy in ~30% of fascicles, C5b-9 sarcolemmal deposition in perifascicular regions). Clinical context = SLE → CONSISTENT WITH SLE-ASSOCIATED INFLAMMATORY MYOSITIS WITH OVERLAP DERMATOMYOSITIS FEATURES ('overlap myositis').

2. MYOSITIS-SPECIFIC ANTIBODY PANEL (returned 3/20):
   - Anti-Jo-1: NEGATIVE
   - Anti-MDA5: NEGATIVE (would have been concerning for rapidly progressive ILD)
   - Anti-TIF1-gamma: NEGATIVE
   - Anti-Mi-2: NEGATIVE
   - Anti-NXP2: NEGATIVE
   - Anti-SAE: NEGATIVE
   - Anti-PM-Scl: NEGATIVE
   - Anti-Ku: NEGATIVE
   - All MSAs and myositis-associated antibodies (MAAs) NEGATIVE.

   The MSA-negative picture in this clinical context further supports SLE-associated myositis (rather than primary DM or anti-synthetase syndrome).

3. SKIN BIOPSY (3/20, FINALIZED 3/22 AM):
   INTERFACE / VACUOLAR DERMATITIS with EPIDERMAL CYTOPLASMIC VACUOLATION, weakly-positive lupus band test, and absence of increased dermal mucin. Interpretation: MIXED process with features of KWASHIORKOR / NUTRITIONAL DERMATOSIS layered on UNDERLYING SLE-ASSOCIATED CUTANEOUS DISEASE.

4. ECHO (HD2, 3/16): LVEF 60%, mild diastolic dysfunction, trace pericardial effusion (no tamponade), no valve vegetations, no regional wall motion abnormalities — no acute cardiac involvement.

5. MBSS (HD3, 3/17): SEVERE oropharyngeal dysphagia with thin-liquid aspiration including silent aspiration on 1 swallow; NPO with NG tube placed.

6. EMG / NCS (HD4, 3/18): Active inflammatory myopathy pattern, proximal-predominant with paraspinal involvement; no neuropathy; no NMJ disorder.

7. PRE-IMMUNOSUPPRESSION INFECTION SCREEN (HD2-3): All NEGATIVE (TB-IGRA, hep B/C, HIV, CMV PCR, EBV PCR, beta-glucan, galactomannan, strongyloides Ab, blood cultures at 5 days).

8. RENAL ASSESSMENT (HD7, completed 3/21): 24-hour urine protein 0.62 g/d; creatinine clearance calculated ~85 mL/min (low-normal — but creatinine of 0.4 is artifactually low due to muscle mass, so true GFR may be lower). Mildly active urine sediment with hyaline casts. RBC casts NOT identified. Lupus nephritis remains possible (early class I or II most likely given pattern). Renal biopsy DEFERRED until further stabilization (platelets, weight, edema).

9. DAILY LABS (3/22 AM):
   - Hgb 8.2 (up from nadir 7.4), WBC 2.85 (up from nadir 2.10), Plt 138 (up from nadir 110); pancytopenia improving.
   - Albumin 1.9 (up from 1.6, slowly trending up with feeding); prealbumin 9 (up from 6, important early marker of nutritional response).
   - K 4.0, Mg 1.9, Phos 2.8 — all repleted, stable on advancing feeds (now at 35 mL/hr = 840 kcal/d as of HD7).
   - CK 1820 (DOWN from peak 2580 on HD3); aldolase 18; LDH 502.
   - Anti-dsDNA 38 (down from 52); C3 68 (up from 56), C4 6 (up from 4) — immunologic activity improving.
   - UPC 0.45 (slowly improving from 0.61).
   - TSH 4.62; free T4 0.9 (improving with increased levothyroxine 50 mcg).
   - LFTs (AST/ALT): AST 78 (down from 142), ALT 52 (down from 86) — consistent with declining muscle inflammation; not hepatotoxic from meds.

ASSESSMENT (HOSPITAL DAY 8):

Integrating clinical, imaging, biopsy, antibody, and laboratory data, we now have diagnostic clarity:

* UNIFYING DIAGNOSIS: ACTIVE SYSTEMIC LUPUS ERYTHEMATOSUS WITH INFLAMMATORY MYOSITIS (overlap myositis pattern with features of both PM and DM, MSA-negative, in clinical context of established SLE), WITH SEVERE PROTEIN-CALORIE MALNUTRITION (KWASHIORKOR-PATTERN) AS A CRITICAL CO-DIAGNOSIS rather than a downstream complication alone.

* CAUSAL CHAIN (most likely): SLE → inflammatory myositis affecting bulbar muscles → progressive oropharyngeal dysphagia → progressive caloric and protein restriction (amplified by food insecurity, language barriers, intermittent access to care, interrupted immunosuppression) → severe protein-calorie malnutrition with kwashiorkor pattern (anasarca, dermatosis, 'flag sign' hair, hypoalbuminemia). The kwashiorkor pattern is NOT merely a complication — it is a major component of her illness with its own treatment imperative (nutritional rehabilitation).

* TREATMENT RESPONSE (early): With cautious nutritional rehabilitation + restarted immunosuppression (prednisone 30 mg + HCQ; MMF restarted 3/19 at 500 mg BID, advanced to 1 g BID 3/21), early signs of response: CK trending down (2580 → 1820), albumin/prealbumin trending up, complements normalizing, dsDNA decreasing, pancytopenia improving, mild strength recovery, edema decreasing.

* GOC: Full code, full medical therapy, family aligned and engaged.

PLAN — HD8 forward:

RHEUMATOLOGY / IMMUNOSUPPRESSION (recommendations endorsed by Dr. Tanaka and Dr. Park, finalized after biopsy results):
1. Today (HD8, 3/22): With muscle biopsy now showing active inflammatory myopathy AND infection screen CLEAR AND patient stable for advance, ESCALATE corticosteroids. Plan to start METHYLPREDNISOLONE 1000 mg IV daily x 3 days (pulse) starting today, with transition back to prednisone 1 mg/kg/day (approximately 36 mg, will use 40 mg for simplicity) on HD11 (3/25), with slow taper over months.
2. Continue mycophenolate 1 g PO BID via NG; plan to increase to 1.5 g BID once stable on pulse steroids. Counseled patient and mother again about teratogenicity and contraception.
3. Continue hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID via NG.
4. Consider adding IVIG if response to pulse steroids is inadequate (planned reassessment HD11-12 prior to next planned pulse if needed). Given the overlap features and dysphagia, IVIG has utility — Dr. Tanaka has reviewed and supports having it on standby.

NUTRITION:
1. Continue current Nutren 1.0 at 35 mL/hr (840 kcal/d). Plan to advance to 45 mL/hr today after AM electrolytes (which look good — K 4.0, Mg 1.9, Phos 2.8).
2. Continue thiamine, multivitamin, folic acid, zinc, vitamin D.
3. Continue strict q6h electrolyte monitoring for 24-48 more hours, then daily.
4. Plan goal feeds 1320-1500 kcal/d by HD12-13.
5. PEG vs. continued NG decision deferred — will reassess swallow on repeat MBSS scheduled HD12 (3/26/2027).

GI:
1. PPI continues.
2. Stool softener as needed; no current concerns.

NEUROLOGY:
1. PT/OT consult ordered for bedside-to-chair work today.
2. Repeat EMG planned outpatient if functional improvement plateaus.

DERMATOLOGY:
1. Continue emollients; gentle skin care.
2. Re-evaluate at 2 weeks (likely outpatient).

PSYCHIATRY CL:
1. Brief intervention planned today. Spanish-speaking psychotherapy ongoing post-discharge.
2. PHQ-9 weekly during admission.

ID:
1. Continue PCP prophylaxis (Bactrim 80/400 mg M/W/F via NG); continue antifungal prophylaxis on pulse steroids: starting fluconazole 200 mg PO daily x duration of pulse + 7 days.
2. Strict aseptic line care.

SOCIAL WORK / CASE MANAGEMENT:
1. Ms. Mercado has filed THIRD Medicaid application with legal aid support. Patient will get 90-day post-discharge emergency Medicaid; pursuing permanent coverage.
2. Discharge planning beginning today: anticipated discharge HD14-18; home setup with mother as primary caregiver; outpatient PCP, rheum, nutrition, SLP, behavioral health all arranged.

PRECAUTIONS / SAFETY:
1. Aspiration precautions ongoing (NPO except crushed meds via NG with water flush).
2. DVT prophylaxis: mechanical only (compression devices) — platelets 138 still low but trending up; will reconsider chemoprophylaxis if platelets >150 stable for 48h.
3. Pressure ulcer prevention; turn q2h; sacral cushion in place.
4. Fall precautions; bedrails up; call light within reach.

FAMILY MEETING:
- Scheduled for 4 PM today with patient, mother, aunt, social work, attending, rheum, nutrition. Will review biopsy results, articulate the unified diagnosis with causal chain, present escalation plan, discuss expected timeline and longer-term outlook. Patient advocate will facilitate in Spanish.

The patient and her family have been remarkable partners in care despite the trauma of the past year. Today represents a turning point: we have diagnostic clarity, the team is aligned on a comprehensive treatment plan, and early biomarkers suggest she is beginning to respond.

Part of the multidisciplinary attendings' synthesis to be conducted at the bedside today during the family meeting.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. David Park, MD (PGY-1) — primary author
Dr. Maria Castro, MD (PGY-3) — co-author and edits
Dr. Sarah Beckman, MD (Attending) — reviewing attending
Date: 3/22/2027
